findings.message: The dependency map still says `BookingDetailsModal` "can reuse for unallocated occupant drill-down", but the live modal is not safely reusable for that path: it always exposes the move UI when `bookingRef` exists and `handleConfirmMoveBooking` reassigns every occupant under the booking, not just the selected occupant. This conflicts with the later read-only-v1 scoping and can mislead analysis back toward the unsafe reuse path.

The test-infrastructure section still lists `pnpm --filter reception test`, but `docs/testing-policy.md:14-17` and `docs/testing-policy.md:28-45` explicitly ban local Jest runs and require CI-only execution. That is stale guidance for the implementer.

The analysis handoff still tells analysis to focus on "modal date semantics for `BookingDetailsModal` reuse", even though this same fact-find now concludes that reuse is blocked and v1 defaults to a read-only panel. Carrying that forward reopens a path the document itself has already ruled unsafe.
